report_name: Injective Labs SDK on npm Hijacked in Crypto-Stealing Supply Chain Attack

threat_actors.name: Injective SDK Attackers
threat_actors.description: The unnamed threat actors responsible for the supply chain attack against the Injective Labs SDK on npm. They compromised the project's GitHub repository to publish a malicious version of the '@injectivelabs/sdk-ts' package.
threat_actors.goals: To steal cryptocurrency private keys and mnemonic seed phrases from developers' systems for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained unauthorized access to the Injective Labs GitHub account or repository, which is part of the software development environment.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used their compromised access to the GitHub repository to publish a trojanized version of the '@injectivelabs/sdk-ts' package to the public npm registry.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: The embedded malware scans the local system of developers for files containing sensitive cryptocurrency data, such as private keys or seed phrases, which are often stored in .env files or wallet backups.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The stolen private keys and seed phrases are exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled server.

malware.name: Injective SDK Infostealer
malware.description: A targeted infostealer embedded within the malicious version of the '@injectivelabs/sdk-ts' npm package. It is designed to find and exfiltrate cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed phrases from developer environments.

tools.name: @injectivelabs/sdk-ts
tools.description: The legitimate Injective Labs SDK package on npm that was compromised and trojanized by attackers to deliver malware.

tools.name: npm
tools.description: The public package registry used by the attackers to distribute the malicious version of the Injective Labs SDK.

tools.name: GitHub
tools.description: The source code repository platform that was compromised, allowing attackers to inject malicious code and gain publishing credentials.

tools.name: Node.js
tools.description: The runtime environment in which the malicious npm package executes its payload on developer systems.

identities.name: Injective Labs
identities.description: The organization whose official SDK for npm, '@injectivelabs/sdk-ts', was compromised in a supply chain attack.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: The software registry that hosted and distributed the malicious version of the '@injectivelabs/sdk-ts' package.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The code hosting platform where the source code repository for the Injective Labs SDK was compromised.

identities.name: Cryptocurrency Developers
identities.description: The downstream targets of the supply chain attack, whose development environments were compromised to steal cryptocurrency wallet keys.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Exfiltration Server
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled server used to receive the exfiltrated private keys and seed phrases from compromised developer systems.

indicators.name: Suspicious npm Package Installation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Injective Labs SDK Installation
id: 9b1d8c4a-7e6f-4b1d-8c4a-9b1d8c4a7e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects the installation of the potentially compromised '@injectivelabs/sdk-ts' npm package. This should be audited to ensure a non-malicious version is in use.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/tag/supply-chain-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'npm'
      - 'install'
      - '@injectivelabs/sdk-ts'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects command-line execution of 'npm install' for the specific Injective Labs SDK package that was compromised.

indicators.name: Unusual Outbound Traffic from Node.js
indicators.pattern:
title: Unusual Outbound Network Connection from Node.js Process
id: f3a2b1c0-9d8e-4f7a-8b6c-5d4e3f2a1b09
status: experimental
description: Monitors for outbound network traffic from Node.js processes, which could indicate exfiltration by a malicious npm package during installation or runtime.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/tag/supply-chain-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1041
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\node.exe'
    Initiated: true
  filter_known_good:
    DestinationHostname|endswith:
      - 'registry.npmjs.org'
      - 'github.com'
  condition: selection and not filter_known_good
indicators.description: Detects outbound network connections from Node.js processes, which could be indicative of the malicious SDK exfiltrating stolen data.

indicators.name: Suspicious npm Post-Install Script Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious npm Post-Install Script Execution
id: 1c2d3e4f-5a6b-4c7d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a 'postinstall.js' script by Node.js, a common vector for malicious npm packages to execute code upon installation.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/tag/supply-chain-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\npm.cmd'
    Image|endswith: '\node.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'postinstall.js'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of a `postinstall.js` script via Node, a common technique used by malicious npm packages to run their payload.

indicators.name: Injective SDK Package Lock File Presence
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Injective SDK Package Lock File Presence
id: a0b1c2d3-e4f5-4a6b-7c8d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of package-lock.json. This file should be reviewed for suspicious versions of '@injectivelabs/sdk-ts' or unexpected new dependencies.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/tag/supply-chain-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: informational
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\package-lock.json'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of package-lock.json, which should be reviewed for suspicious versions of the compromised SDK.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

relationships.source_name: Injective SDK Package Lock File Presence
